subject: Re: For Mr. Jeffrey Epstein  Blood Diamond Movie and Dinner
body:
ask her to reserve two seats

From: Peggy Siegal [mailto:peggy@peggysiegal.com] 
Sent: Monday, November 27, 2006 3:07 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: For Mr. Jeffrey Epstein Blood Diamond Movie and Dinner


 
ED ZWICK
INVITES YOU TO CELEBRATE THE CAST
LEONARDO DICAPRIO
JENNIFER CONNELLY   DJIMON HOUNSOU
AT A PRIVATE SCREENING OF
WARNER BROS. PICTURES AND VIRTUAL STUDIOS
BLOOD DIAMOND
THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 30, 2006
7:00PM ROY & NIUTA TITUS I THEATER
THE MUSEUM OF MODERN ART
11 WEST 53RD STREET
ENTER AT THE RONALD S. AND JO CAROLE LAUDER CANOPY
9:00PM LE CIRQUE
150 EAST 58TH STREET
BETWEEN LEXINGTON & THIRD AVENUES
Confirmed guests
Richard Belzer
Marshall Brickman
Jennifer Connelly
Damon Dash & Rachel Roy
Dana Delany
Leonardo Dicaprio
Peter Dinklage
Noah Emmerich
Bert Fields
Sandy Gallin
Terry George
Djimon Hounsou
Colin Hanks
James Hoge
Lauren Hutton
Katie Joel
Henry Kravis
Damien Loeb
Michael Nouri
Piper Perabo
Steven Shainberg
Elizabeth Vargas
Mike Wallace
George C Wolfe
PEGGY@PEGGYSIEGAL.COM OR 212-935-6700
THIS INVITATION IS NON-TRANSFERABLE
Set against the backdrop of the chaos and civil war that enveloped 1990s Sierra Leone , Blood Diamond is the story of Danny Archer (Leonardo DiCaprio), a South African mercenary, and Solomon Vandy (Djimon Hounsou), a Mende fisherman. Both men are African, but their histories and their circumstances are as different as any can be-until their fates become joined in a common quest to recover a rare pink diamond that can transform their lives. While in prison for smuggling, Archer learns that Solomon-who was taken from his family and forced to work in the diamond fields-has found and hidden the extraordinary rough stone. With the help of Maddy Bowen (Jennifer Connelly), an American journalist whose idealism is tempered by a deepening connection with Archer, the two men embark on a trek through rebel territory. More than a search for a valuable diamond, the journey could save Solomon’s family and give Archer the second chance he thought he would never have.

 
This email was sent to lesley@nysgllc.com, by peggy@peggysiegal.com
